overview: This Legacy Tron variant leverages massive mana acceleration to overwhelm opponents before they can stabilize. By combining The One Ring and Grim Monolith with traditional Tron lands, you generate exponential resources to deploy Candelabra of Tawnos. The deck aims to flood the board with tokens or cast oversized threats that dictate the tempo of the game.
win_conditions: Generate exponential mana to cast oversized threats early
Use Candelabra of Tawnos to create unblockable token armies
Leverage The One Ring and Grim Monolith for early game card advantage and acceleration

keep_criteria: Hands with multiple Tron lands and The One Ring
Hands containing Grim Monolith and early mana sources
Hands with Candelabra of Tawnos and sufficient mana to cast it
Keep any hand with 2+ lands and a mana acceleration piece (Sol Ring, Arcane Signet, Chalice of the Void) or a Tron piece.
Keep hands with 3+ lands if you have no acceleration but have fetch lands.
Against combo, keep hands with 2+ lands and interaction (Surgical Strike, Dismember).
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking acceleration or card draw
Hands missing The One Ring or Grim Monolith
Mulligan hands with 0-1 lands, or 3+ lands with no acceleration/Tron pieces.

early_game: Prioritize casting The One Ring and Grim Monolith to establish early mana and card advantage
Play Tron lands and search for them if possible
Avoid unnecessary combat damage while ramping
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Sol Ring/Arcane Signet/Chalice, or fetch a dual land.
Turn 2: Play a land, cast a Tron piece or acceleration, or fetch another land.
Prioritize untapped lands when possible, but never skip casting acceleration for a tapped land.
Sequence fetches to find Shivan Reef or Ancient Tomb for early mana, then play Tron pieces.
If you hit Tron on turn 2 or 3, cast your threat immediately.
mid_game: Cast Candelabra of Tawnos once mana is sufficient
Begin applying pressure with tokens or mid-sized threats
never hoard mana past turn 3 unless you're holding up counterspells or waiting for a specific threat.
Use Candelabra of Tawnos to recycle lands for extra mana, but be mindful of the 2-mana cost.
Protect your Tron pieces with Chalice of the Void (set to 0 or 1) and countermagic.
Graveyard is generally irrelevant unless facing Rest in Peace
late_game: Flood the board with Candelabra tokens
Cast game-ending threats or combo pieces
Control the board with excess resources
Ugin, the Spirit Dragon is the primary win condition, providing card advantage and board wipe.
If Ugin is dead or you're ahead, cast a second Ugin or use your mana to attack with creatures if applicable.
In the mirror, winning the resource war and out-resourcing with Ugin or a second Tron turn is key.
Go all-in when you have assembled Tron, have a threat in hand, and your opponent has no clear answer in hand or on board.
If you're ahead on board, commit to attacking and casting your threat to close the game.
If you're behind, commit to casting your threat immediately to stabilize.

key_cards.name: Candelabra of Tawnos
key_cards.role: Primary win condition and board flood
key_cards.notes: Cast once mana is established to create 1-4 1/1 black creature tokens, overwhelming blockers and enabling combat tricks

key_cards.name: Grim Monolith
key_cards.role: Early game acceleration and card draw
key_cards.notes: Use to untap lands and draw cards, bridging the gap to Candelabra while maintaining tempo

key_cards.name: The One Ring
key_cards.notes: Tap for colorless mana and draw a card, serving as a consistent engine to fuel the Tron land synergy

sequencing_tips: Prioritize The One Ring and Grim Monolith before casting Candelabra to maximize mana efficiency
Hold up mana for countermagic or removal if opponent plays early threats
Sequence land drops to ensure Candelabra can be cast on curve or turn after
Use Grim Monolith's draw ability to find answers before committing to Candelabra
Tron is highly sensitive to land drops
always prioritize playing lands over casting spells if you're behind on mana.
Candelabra of Tawnos is incredibly powerful but requires careful mana management
The One Ring is a powerful card advantage engine but can be a liability against Rest in Peace
consider mulliganing if you expect heavy graveyard hate.

matchups.advice: Use The One Ring and Grim Monolith to stabilize early, then flood the board with Candelabra tokens to outvalue the opponent
Race with Candelabra tokens before opponent sets up permanent countermagic, leveraging Grim Monolith for card advantage
Disrupt their sequence with early pressure from Candelabra tokens, using The One Ring to draw into interaction

Use Candelabra of Tawnos to generate mana for multiple threats or to cast your threat in response to a counterspell. Set Chalice to 0 to counter their cheap counterspells. Use fetch lands to find untapped lands to cast threats in response to counterspells. If they have multiple counters, consider casting your threat at end step or using a cheap threat like Ugin (which is a planeswalker, not a spell, so it bypasses counterspells).

Out-accelerate them by prioritizing Tron assembly. Use Cursed Totem to destroy their mana rocks. If they go wide, use Engineered Explosives set to 2 or 3. Ugin's -3 loyalty ability can remove key threats.

Win the resource war. Prioritize land drops and Tron assembly. Use countermagic to protect your threats and disrupt theirs. Ugin is the key; winning the planeswalker war usually decides the match. Be willing to trade Tron pieces for theirs if it gives you a tempo advantage.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells (Mana Leak, Spell Pierce, Daze), targeted removal (Surgical Strike, Dismember), and sweepers (Engineered Explosives, Cursed Totem).
Pay attention to how many lands they play
control decks often play extra lands to stabilize.
Note if they're holding up mana for multiple turns, indicating a counterspell or sweeper.
